name: Tokitsukaze and Mahjong
input: The only line contains three strings — the tiles in Tokitsukaze's hand. For each string, the first character is a digit ranged from $1$ to $9$ and the second character is m, p or s.
output: Print a single integer — the minimum number of extra suited tiles she needs to draw.
note:
In the first example, Tokitsukaze already has a shuntsu.
In the second example, Tokitsukaze already has a koutsu.
In the third example, Tokitsukaze can get a shuntsu by drawing one suited tile — 1p or 4p. The resulting tiles will be [3p, 9m, 2p, 1p] or [3p, 9m, 2p, 4p].
